sections.content:

<function=python>

    b = n['boundary']

    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}], children={n['children']}")

print("\nAround divider:")
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
for r in range(14, 25):
    print(f"  {r}: {ascii_lines[r][20:45]}")
</parameter>
</function>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see what's happening more clearly:
1. The black character (B) has crossed to the RIGHT side of the divider (cols 30-38)
2. The dark gray character (c) is now on the LEFT side (cols 24-29) but reduced to 36 pixels (from 45)
3. The black character now contains white dots AND some light blue (S) pixels as children
4. The divider (S) is split - top part (45 pixels) and bottom part (135 pixels)
Looking at the ASCII:
- Row 15-17: Dark gray (c) on left, black (B) on right of divider
- Row 18+: Dark gray (c) vertical bar on left, divider (S) in middle, black (B) L-shape on right
So the characters have swapped sides! The black character moved from left to right, and the dark gray character moved from right to left. They passed through each other at the divider.
The black character now has some S (light blue) pixels as children (id=7), which suggests it picked up some of the divider color.
The dark gray character is now at cols 24-29 on the left side, with 36 pixels (lost 9 from original 45).
The yellow L-shape is still at the bottom right (45 pixels).
I think the goal might be about matching the black character's position/shape to the yellow L-shape, or perhaps the dark gray character needs to be fully consumed.
Wait, let me reconsider. The black character started as an L-shape facing right (horizontal bar on top, vertical bar going down on the right). The dark gray character was also an L-shape but facing left (horizontal bar on top, vertical bar going down on the left). The yellow shape is an L-shape facing right (horizontal bar on top, vertical bar going down on the left).
After moving RIGHT 4 times, the black character is now on the right side and the dark gray is on the left. The characters have essentially swapped positions.
Let me think about what the goal could be:
1. Maybe I need to position the black character to match the yellow L-shape
2. Maybe I need to completely consume the dark gray character
3. Maybe the game involves moving between the two sides
Let me try pressing LEFT to see if the characters move back, and also try other directions to understand the full movement model.

Goal model: Still unclear. Could be about positioning the black character to match the yellow L-shape, or consuming the dark gray character.
Action model: RIGHT moves black character right through divider, LEFT should reverse. Need to test other directions.
Plan: Try LEFT to reverse the movement, then explore other directions.
